column1: # Format: label<TAB>handleA<TAB>handleB. Blank lines and #-comments ignored.
# Handles may contain spaces, so the separator is a tab.
# The label is a GROUND-TRUTH human judgment of whether the two handles would
# plausibly be confused over a noisy radio channel — NOT a record of what the
# checker currently reports. Pairs the engine gets wrong belong here too (they
# are what the precision/recall floors in TestConfusabilityCorpus measure, and
# what future engine work should fix). Do not relabel a pair to make the test
# pass; fix the engine or adjust the floors with a comment.
#   confusable — a reasonable operator could mistake one for the other on air;
#                the checker should flag the pair at MEDIUM or above.
#   distinct   — clearly distinguishable by ear; the checker should stay below
# When adding pairs, prefer real proposed handles and real confusions from the
# --- vowel-quality differences (the reason espeak-ng is preferred) ---
# --- alike at both ends, or sharing a word ---
# Borderline: same stressed /ar/ nucleus, shared middle /m/, only a faint
# trailing /t/ and the m/k onset apart — a MEDIUM "worth a look" is right.
# --- distinct: moderate global distance but no real shared run ---
# --- distinct: partial overlaps that should not count ---
# Known engine noise: /i/ vs /ɑ/ is a maximally distant vowel pair and vowels
# survive channel noise well, but Metaphone's vowel-collapsing escalation rates
# the pair a HIGH sound-alike and the coarse binary vowel features let the
# phoneme MED band fire too (see IMPROVEMENTS.md §2 "Metaphone fallback
# over-fires on vowels" and §5 "graded vowel distances").
